heroHeadline: Day 48: CJNG Fractures — El Jardinero & El Sapo Declare War on O3 and Los Cuinis as Semana Santa Ends
heroSubtitle: Day 48 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. In a dramatic escalation, El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) and El Sapo have formally declared war on O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) and Los Cuinis — a 'fractura total' in CJNG's command structure timed precisely to coincide with the end of Operativo Semana Santa Segura 2026 (concludes April 12). Cartel-aligned media circulates the unverified claim that O3 and Los Cuinis 'handed over' El Mencho to authorities. O3 retains Grupo Élite's paramilitary firepower; El Jardinero commands broad regional plazas in Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, and Michoacán. With 61 days to the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match (June 11), the fracture represents a major new security threat variable for Guadalajara. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 67+ officials detained.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, France 24, CGTN, Al Jazeera, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 11, 2026.
